failure_modes.text: Teams invoke survivorship bias abstractly but still use samples or case studies that exclude the hidden denominator.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when teams invoke survivorship bias abstractly but still use samples, benchmarks, or case studies that exclude the hidden denominator.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit the sample for the hidden denominator: before drawing conclusions from success stories, check what fraction of similar attempts failed and whether those failures are represented.

failure_modes.text: The correction fails when it becomes so broad that meaningful differences between actual winners and losers are ignored.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the anti-bias correction becomes so broad that meaningful differences between real winners and real losers are ignored rather than analyzed.
failure_modes.mitigation: Preserve meaningful signal: when correcting for survivorship bias, still analyze what the winners did differently — the correction should refine the lesson, not erase it.

failure_modes.text: Success stories can become an availability trap that substitutes a memorable survivor narrative for the real outcome distribution.
failure_modes.mitigation: Counter availability with base rates: when a memorable success story dominates judgment, check the full outcome distribution to see whether the case is representative or an outlier.

premortem_questions.text: What non-survivors, failures, or hidden cases are missing from this sample?
Are we overcorrecting so much that real differences between winners and losers disappear?
What outside-view base rate would this conclusion look like if the vivid survivor story were removed?

heuristics.text: Bring the hidden denominator into the evidence base before trusting the lesson drawn from survivors.
Use the outside view and base rates to counter the pull of vivid success stories.
Analyze real differences between survivors and non-survivors instead of treating the warning as a reason to flatten everything.
heuristics.source_quote: meaningful differences between real winners and real losers

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on hidden-denominator correction, outside-view base rates, and anti-success-story distortion.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor statistics sprawl because the raw source is stronger on denominator recovery and caution against winner-only evidence.
open_questions: The first heuristic compresses several source passages on benchmark and sample design so the chunk stays operational.

allies: The raw ally surface is strongest on critical-thinking, base-rate style outside view, and premortem-like team challenge as later relation material.
antagonists: The raw antagonist pressure is strongest on confirmation-bias and optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy, but only clean current-vocabulary ids should survive Wave 3.
structured_tensions: The source has explicit tension blocks with confirmation-bias and optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy that cleanly map into the current target-id surface.
